info.title: Are.na API
info.description:
The Are.na REST API provides programmatic access to the Are.na platform - a tool for connecting ideas and building knowledge together.

## Getting Started

All API requests use `https://api.are.na` as the base URL. Responses are JSON with consistent structure including hypermedia links for resource discovery.

## Authentication

Most endpoints work without authentication but provide additional access when authenticated. Use the standard `Authorization` header:

```
Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN
```

**Supported Token Types:**
- **OAuth2 Access Token**: Obtained via [OAuth2 flow](https://www.are.na/developers/explore/authentication) (supports PKCE)
- **Personal Access Token**: From [are.na/settings/oauth](https://www.are.na/settings/personal-access-tokens)

For OAuth2, note that the authorization endpoint is hosted on the main site (`www.are.na/oauth/authorize`),
while the token endpoint is on the API (`api.are.na/v3/oauth/token`). The authorization endpoint supports
`read` and `write` scopes (defaulting to `read` if omitted). A typical authorization URL looks like:

```
https://www.are.na/oauth/authorize?client_id=YOUR_CLIENT_ID&redirect_uri=YOUR_REDIRECT_URI&response_type=code&scope=read
```

**Authentication Levels:**
- **Public**: No authentication needed (e.g., `/v3/ping`)
- **Optional**: Works unauthenticated but respects permissions when authenticated
- **Required**: Returns `401 Unauthorized` without valid token (e.g., `/v3/me`)

## Scopes

OAuth2 tokens and personal access tokens support the following scopes:

| Scope | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `read` | Read-only access (default) — write operations will return `403 Forbidden` |
| `write` | Full read and write access |

Tokens default to `read` scope. Request `write` scope when creating a token or authorizing an OAuth2 application to enable write access. Read-only tokens can view all resources the user has access to but cannot create, update, or delete anything.

## Rate Limiting

**Acceptable Use**: This API is intended for building applications that integrate with Are.na, not for scraping or bulk data collection. Automated crawling, systematic downloading of content, or any form of structured data harvesting is prohibited. If you need bulk access to data for research or other purposes, please [contact us](mailto:help@are.na) to discuss your use case.

Rate limits are enforced per-minute based on your tier:

| Tier | Requests/Minute |
|------|-----------------|
| Guest (unauthenticated) | 30 |
| Free | 120 |
| Premium | 300 |
| Supporter/Lifetime | 600 |

**Rate Limit Headers** (included in every response):
- `X-RateLimit-Limit`: Your tier's per-minute limit
- `X-RateLimit-Tier`: Current tier (guest/free/premium/supporter)
- `X-RateLimit-Window`: Time window in seconds (always 60)
- `X-RateLimit-Reset`: Unix timestamp when the limit resets

When you exceed limits, you'll receive a `429 Too Many Requests` response with upgrade recommendations and retry timing.

## Request Validation

All parameters are validated against this OpenAPI specification. Invalid requests return `400 Bad Request` with detailed error messages.

## Error Responses

Errors use standard HTTP status codes with JSON bodies:

```json
{
  "error": "Not Found",
  "code": 404,
  "details": {
    "message": "The resource you requested does not exist"
  }
}
```

Common status codes:
- `400`: Invalid request parameters
- `401`: Authentication required or invalid
- `403`: Insufficient permissions
- `404`: Resource not found
- `429`: Rate limit exceeded

## Pagination

List endpoints return paginated results. Use these query parameters:
- `page`: Page number (default: 1)
- `per`: Items per page (default: 24, max: 100)

Responses include a `meta` object with pagination info:

```json
{
  "data": [...],
  "meta": {
    "current_page": 1,
    "per_page": 25,
    "total_pages": 5,
    "total_count": 120,
    "next_page": 2,
    "prev_page": null,
    "has_more_pages": true
  }
}
```

## Best Practices

Are.na channels and users can contain thousands of items. The following guidelines will help you build responsive, well-behaved integrations.

### Paginate, Don't Enumerate

Never try to load an entire channel or user's content in one pass. Use `page` and `per` parameters to fetch only the data you need right now. Check `meta.has_more_pages` and load subsequent pages on demand rather than in a loop.

**Do:**
- Fetch the first page and display it immediately
- Load more pages as the user scrolls or explicitly requests them

**Don't:**
- Loop through all pages at startup to "build a complete picture"
- Set `per=100` and iterate until `has_more_pages` is `false`

### Use HTTP Caching

All responses include `Cache-Control` and `ETag` headers. Unauthenticated responses are `max-age=300, public` — your client can reuse them for 5 minutes without any network request. Authenticated responses are `private, no-cache` — your client may store them but must revalidate before each reuse.

To revalidate, send the `ETag` value from a previous response as `If-None-Match`:

```
GET /v3/channels/123
If-None-Match: "abc123"
```

If the resource hasn't changed, the API returns `304 Not Modified` with no body, saving bandwidth. Single-resource endpoints (e.g., `/v3/channels/:id`, `/v3/blocks/:id`) also skip server-side work entirely on a match. Note that 304 responses still count against your rate limit.

### Fetch Summaries Before Details

Start with lightweight list endpoints (e.g., a channel's first page of contents) before drilling into individual blocks or nested resources. Avoid eagerly resolving every connection or nested object — fetch details only when a user explicitly needs them.

### Respect Rate Limits Gracefully

Monitor the `X-RateLimit-Limit` and `X-RateLimit-Reset` headers. If you receive a `429` response, wait until the reset window before retrying — don't retry in a tight loop. Consider adding exponential backoff and a short delay between sequential requests (e.g., 200–500ms) when making multiple calls.

### Design for Partial Data

Channels can have tens of thousands of connections. Your application should function well with just the first page of results, not require the full dataset upfront. Display what you have and offer the user a way to load more.

### Build Statically When Possible

If you're building a website that displays Are.na content, prefer static site generation (fetching data at build time) over live API requests on every page view. This eliminates rate limit concerns for your visitors and makes your site faster and more resilient. If you need fresher data, cache responses server-side with a reasonable TTL rather than proxying every request through to the API.

### Avoid Write-Heavy Loops

If you're creating or connecting multiple blocks, batch your logic and add delays between write operations. Rapid-fire mutations can hit rate limits quickly and may trigger abuse detection.

BearerAuth.description:
Bearer token authentication.

Accepts two token types:
- OAuth2 access tokens (obtained via OAuth2 flow with Doorkeeper)
- Personal access tokens (from your account settings at are.na/settings)

Example: `Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN`

OAuth2.description:
OAuth 2.0 authentication. See [RFC 6749](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6749) for details.

**Supported Flows:**
- **Authorization Code**: For web and mobile apps with a backend
- **Authorization Code + PKCE**: For public clients (mobile apps, SPAs) without a client secret
- **Client Credentials**: For server-to-server integrations

PKCE ([RFC 7636](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7636)) is supported for enhanced security with
public clients. Use `code_challenge_method=S256` for best security.

Access tokens do not expire. Register your application at [are.na/oauth/applications](https://www.are.na/oauth/applications).

scopes.write: Full read and write access to your account
Full read and write access to your account

ConnectionSortParam.name: sort
ConnectionSortParam.description: Sort by the date the relationship was created.

ContentSortParam.name: sort
ContentSortParam.description: Sort by creation or last update time.

GroupSortParam.name: sort
GroupSortParam.description: Sort groups by name or date.

ChannelContentSortParam.name: sort
ChannelContentSortParam.description:
Sort channel contents. Use `position` for the owner's manual
arrangement, or sort by date. Defaults to `position_desc`.

ContentTypeFilterParam.name: type
ContentTypeFilterParam.description: Filter to a specific content type.

ForbiddenResponse.description: Forbidden - insufficient permissions to access this resource

BadRequestResponse.description: Bad request - missing or invalid parameters

UnprocessableEntityResponse.description: Validation failed - the request was well-formed but contained semantic errors

message.example: The resource you are looking for does not exist.

RateLimitError.description: Rate limit exceeded error response with upgrade information and suggestions

message.example: Rate limit of 30 requests per minute exceeded for guest tier. Try again later.

limit.description: Request limit per minute for this tier

retry_after.description: Suggested seconds to wait before retrying

example: Sign up for a free account to get 120 requests per minute
Cache responses when possible to reduce API calls

headers_note.example: Check 'X-RateLimit-*' headers on successful requests for current usage

Links.description:
HATEOAS links for navigation and discovery.
Follows HAL (Hypertext Application Language) format where link relationships 
are expressed as object keys (e.g., "self", "user", "channels").

self.description: Link to the current resource (always present)

Link.description:
A hypermedia link containing the URL of a linked resource.
The relationship type is expressed by the key in the parent _links object.

href.description: The URL of the linked resource

html.example: This is only a test.

plain.description: Plain text rendering of the markdown

EmbeddedUser.description: Embedded user representation (used when user is nested in other resources)

slug.description: URL-safe identifier (use this in API paths)

Metadata.description:
Arbitrary key-value pairs stored on an entity.
Keys are alphanumeric/underscore, max 40 characters.
Values are scalars (string, number, boolean). Max 50 keys, 32KB total.

MetadataInput.description:
Arbitrary key-value pairs to set on an entity. Uses merge semantics:
new keys are added, existing keys are updated, keys set to null are removed.
Keys must be alphanumeric/underscore, max 40 characters.
Values must be scalars (string, number, boolean) or null (to delete). Max 50 keys, 32KB total.

EmbeddedConnection.description: Embedded connection representation used when connection is nested in other resources

position.description: Position of the item within the channel (1-indexed)

Connection.description:
Full connection resource with abilities and links.
Used for GET /v3/connections/:id

ConnectionAbilities.description: Actions the current user can perform on this connection

remove.description: Whether the user can remove this connection

EmbeddedGroup.description: Embedded group representation (used when group is nested in other resources)

updated_at.description: When the user was last updated

bio.description: User biography with markdown, HTML, and plain text renderings

UserCounts.description: Counts of various items for the user

channels.description: Number of channels owned by the user

bio.description: Group biography with markdown, HTML, and plain text renderings

updated_at.description: When the group was last updated

GroupCounts.description: Counts of various items for the group

channels.description: Number of channels owned by the group

users.description: Number of users in the group

UserBadge.description:
Denotes plan or other distinction:
- `staff`: Are.na staff member
- `investor`: Investor
- `supporter`: Supporter subscriber
- `premium`: Premium subscriber

UserTier.description:
User subscription tier:
- `guest`: Unauthenticated user
- `free`: Free account
- `premium`: Premium subscriber
- `supporter`: Supporter tier

BlockState.description:
Processing state of a block.
- `processing`: Being processed (e.g., image resizing)
- `available`: Ready for display
- `failed`: Processing failed

BlockVisibility.description:
Visibility of a block.
- `public`: Visible to everyone
- `private`: Only visible to owner
- `orphan`: Not connected to any channel

ChannelState.description:
Lifecycle state of a channel.
- `available`: Active and accessible
- `deleted`: Soft deleted

ConnectionFilter.description:
Filter connections by who created them.
- `ALL`: All connections (default)
- `OWN`: Only connections by the current user
- `EXCLUDE_OWN`: Exclude connections by the current user

FollowableType.description:
Type of entity that can be followed.
- `User`: A user
- `Channel`: A channel
- `Group`: A group

ConnectableType.description:
Type of entity that can be connected to a channel.
- `Block`: A block
- `Channel`: A channel

ContentTypeFilter.description:
Filter by content type.
- `Text`: Text blocks
- `Image`: Image blocks
- `Link`: Link blocks
- `Attachment`: File attachments
- `Embed`: Embedded media (video, audio, etc.)
- `Channel`: Channels only
- `Block`: All block types (excludes channels)

SearchTypeFilter.description:
Filter by content type. Includes users and groups.
- `All`: Everything (default)
- `Text`, `Image`, `Link`, `Attachment`, `Embed`: Block types
- `Block`: All block types
- `Channel`: Channels only
- `User`: Users only
- `Group`: Groups only

FileExtension.description:
File extension for filtering attachment and image blocks.
Common values: pdf, jpg, png, gif, mp4, mp3, doc, xls.

ConnectionSort.description:
Sort order for relationship lists.
- `created_at_desc`: Newest first (default)
- `created_at_asc`: Oldest first

ChannelContentSort.description:
Sort order for channel contents.
- `position_asc`: Manual order (default)
- `position_desc`: Manual order, reversed
- `created_at_desc`: Newest first
- `created_at_asc`: Oldest first
- `updated_at_desc`: Recently updated first
- `updated_at_asc`: Least recently updated first

ContentSort.description:
Sort order for user or group content.
- `created_at_desc`: Newest first (default)
- `created_at_asc`: Oldest first
- `updated_at_desc`: Recently updated first
- `updated_at_asc`: Least recently updated first

GroupSort.description:
Sort order for groups.
- `name_asc`: Alphabetical (default)
- `name_desc`: Reverse alphabetical
- `created_at_desc`: Newest first
- `created_at_asc`: Oldest first
- `updated_at_desc`: Recently updated first
- `updated_at_asc`: Least recently updated first

SearchScope.description:
Limit search to a specific context.
- `all`: Everything accessible to the user (default)
- `my`: Only the current user's own content
- `following`: Content from followed users and channels

SearchSort.description:
Sort order for search results.
- `score_desc`: Most relevant first (default)
- `created_at_desc`: Newest first
- `created_at_asc`: Oldest first
- `updated_at_desc`: Recently updated first
- `updated_at_asc`: Least recently updated first
- `name_asc`: Alphabetical A-Z
- `name_desc`: Alphabetical Z-A
- `connections_count_desc`: Most connected first
- `random`: Random (use `seed` for reproducibility)

ChannelVisibility.description:
Visibility level of a channel:
- `public`: Anyone can view and connect to the channel
- `private`: Only the owner and collaborators can view
- `closed`: Anyone can view, but only collaborators can add content

Movement.description:
Movement action for repositioning a connection within a channel.
"Top" refers to the visually first position (newest items appear at the top).
- `insert_at`: Move to a specific position (requires `position` parameter)
- `move_to_top`: Move to the visually first position
- `move_to_bottom`: Move to the visually last position
- `move_up`: Move one position up (towards the top)
- `move_down`: Move one position down (towards the bottom)

ChannelIds.description:
Array of channel IDs or slugs. Accepts numeric IDs, string IDs, or
channel slugs (e.g., `[123, "456", "my-channel-slug"]`).

ConnectTo.description: A channel to connect to, with optional position and connection metadata.

position.description: Position to insert at within this channel (1-indexed).

metadata.description: Connection metadata for this specific connection.

PresignedFile.description: A presigned S3 upload URL for a single file

upload_url.description:
Presigned S3 PUT URL. Upload your file by sending a PUT request
to this URL with the file bytes as the body and the `Content-Type`
header matching the content_type you specified.

key.description: The S3 object key where the file will be stored

content_type.description: The content type that was validated and should be used in the PUT request

PresignResponse.description: Response containing presigned S3 upload URLs

files.description: Presigned URLs for each requested file

expires_in.description: Seconds until the presigned URLs expire

BlockInput.description: Input fields for creating a block

value.description:
The content to create a block from. Can be either:
- A URL (creates Image, Link, or Embed block based on content type)
- Text/markdown content (creates a Text block)

metadata.description: Custom key-value metadata to set on the new block.

BatchResponse.description: Response returned when a batch is accepted for processing

total.description: Total number of blocks queued for creation

BatchStatus.description: Current status of a batch processing job

total.description: Total number of blocks in the batch

successful_count.description: Number of blocks created so far

index.description: Original index in the request array
Original index in the request array

completed_at.description: When the batch finished processing (present when completed or failed)

error.description: Top-level error message if the entire batch failed

Block.description:
A block is a piece of content on Are.na. Blocks come in different types,
each with its own set of fields. Use the `type` field to determine which
fields are available.

BaseBlockProperties.description: Common properties shared by all block types

comment_count.description: Number of comments on the block

updated_at.description: When the block was last updated

source.description: Source URL and metadata (if block was created from a URL)

connection.description:
Connection context (only present when block is returned as part of channel contents).
Contains position, pinned status, and information about who connected the block.

can.description:
Abilities object (only present for full block responses, not in channel contents).
Indicates what actions the current user can perform on this block.

BlockAbilities.description: Actions the current user can perform on the block

manage.description: Whether the user can manage (update/delete) this block

comment.description: Whether the user can comment on this block

connect.description: Whether the user can connect this block to channels

TextBlock.description: A text block containing markdown content

content.description: Text content with markdown, HTML, and plain text renderings

ImageBlock.description: An image block containing an uploaded or scraped image

LinkBlock.description: A link block representing a URL with optional preview

content.description: Extracted text content from the link

AttachmentBlock.description: An attachment block containing an uploaded file

image.description: Preview image (for PDFs and other previewable files)

EmbedBlock.description: An embed block containing embedded media (video, audio, etc.)

embed.description: Embed data including HTML and dimensions

PendingBlock.description:
A block that is currently being processed. The final type (Text, Image, Link, etc.)
will be determined once processing completes. Check the `state` field for processing status.

type.description: Block type (always "PendingBlock" for blocks being processed)

url.description: Provider URL (from parsed URI scheme and host)

alt_text.description: Alternative text associated with the image

blurhash.description: BlurHash representation of the image for progressive loading

updated_at.description: When the image was last updated

ImageVersion.description: A resized/processed version of an image with multiple resolution URLs

src_2x.description: 2x resolution image URL for high DPI displays

width.description: Width of the resized image in pixels

height.description: Height of the resized image in pixels

updated_at.description: When the attachment was last updated

body.description: Comment body with markdown, HTML, and plain text renderings

updated_at.description: When the comment was last updated
When the channel was last updated

collaborators.description:
Collaborators on this channel (users and groups).
Only present when channel is returned as a full resource, not when embedded.

connection.description:
Connection context (only present when channel is returned as part of another channel's contents).
Contains position, pinned status, and information about who connected the channel.

can.description:
Actions the current user can perform on this channel.
Only present when channel is returned as a full resource, not when embedded.

ChannelAbilities.description: Actions the current user can perform on the channel

add_to.description: Whether the user can add blocks to this channel

update.description: Whether the user can update this channel

destroy.description: Whether the user can delete this channel

manage_collaborators.description: Whether the user can add/remove collaborators

ChannelCounts.description: Counts of various items in the channel

blocks.description: Number of blocks in the channel

channels.description: Number of channels connected to this channel

collaborators.description: Number of collaborators on the channel

next_page.description: Next page number (null if last page)

prev_page.description: Previous page number (null if first page)

has_more_pages.description: Whether there are more pages available

PaginatedResponse.description: Base schema for all paginated responses (use allOf to extend with specific data type)

UserList.description: Data payload containing an array of users

ChannelList.description: Data payload containing an array of channels

ConnectableList.description: Data payload containing mixed content that can be connected to channels (blocks and channels)

FollowableList.description: Data payload containing followable items (users, channels, and groups)

data.description: Array of users, channels, and/or groups

EverythingList.description: Data payload containing all content types

data.description: Array of results (blocks, channels, users, or groups)

UserListResponse.description: Paginated list of users with total count

ChannelListResponse.description: Paginated list of channels with total count

GroupList.description: Data payload containing an array of groups

GroupListResponse.description: Paginated list of groups with total count

ConnectableListResponse.description: Paginated list of connectable content (blocks and channels)

FollowableListResponse.description: Paginated list of followable items (users, channels, and groups) with total count

EverythingListResponse.description: Paginated list of all content types with total count

CommentList.description: Data payload containing an array of comments

CommentListResponse.description: Paginated list of comments with total count

post.description:
Exchange credentials for an access token. This is the OAuth 2.0 token endpoint.

**Supported Grant Types:**

- `authorization_code`: Exchange an authorization code for an access token
- `authorization_code` + PKCE: For public clients without a client secret
- `client_credentials`: Authenticate as your application (server-to-server)

**PKCE Support:** For public clients (mobile apps, SPAs), use PKCE:
1. Generate a random `code_verifier` (43-128 chars, alphanumeric + `-._~`)
2. Create `code_challenge` = Base64URL(SHA256(code_verifier))
3. In the authorization request to `https://www.are.na/oauth/authorize`, include:
   - `code_challenge`: The generated challenge
   - `code_challenge_method`: `S256`
4. When exchanging the code at this endpoint, include `code_verifier`

See [RFC 7636](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7636) for details.

Access tokens do not expire and can be used indefinitely. Register your application
at [are.na/oauth/applications](https://www.are.na/oauth/applications) to obtain client credentials.

client_id.description: Your application's client ID (required for all grant types)

client_secret.description: Your application's client secret (required for confidential clients, omit for PKCE)

redirect_uri.description: Redirect URI used in authorization request (required for authorization_code grant)

code_verifier.description:
PKCE code verifier (required when authorization used code_challenge).
Must be 43-128 characters from [A-Z], [a-z], [0-9], "-", ".", "_", "~".

authorization_code.description: Exchange authorization code for token (confidential client)

authorization_code_pkce.description: Exchange authorization code for token (public client with PKCE)

access_token.description: The access token to use for API requests

created_at.description: Unix timestamp when the token was created

value.error_description: The provided authorization grant is invalid, expired, or revoked.
The authorization grant type is not supported.

error_description.example: Client authentication failed due to unknown client or invalid credentials.

get.description: Returns the OpenAPI 3.0 specification for this API in YAML format. This endpoint provides the complete API contract for programmatic access and documentation generation.
Returns the OpenAPI 3.0 specification for this API in JSON format. This endpoint provides the complete API contract in JSON for tools that prefer JSON over YAML.
Public utility endpoint for API health checks and connection testing.

X-RateLimit-Limit.description: Request limit per minute for this user's tier

post.description:
Returns presigned S3 PUT URLs for direct file upload. Use this to upload
files (images, attachments) without sending them through the API server.
Supports up to 50 files per request.

**Upload flow:**
1. Call this endpoint with an array of files (filename + content type)
2. PUT each file's bytes to its returned `upload_url` with the matching `Content-Type` header
3. Create blocks via `POST /v3/blocks` with `value` set to the S3 URL:
   `https://s3.amazonaws.com/arena_images-temp/<key>`

Presigned URLs expire after 1 hour.

**Authentication required.**

files.description: Array of files to generate presigned URLs for

filename.description: Name of the file to upload

post.description:
Creates a new block and connects it to one or more channels.

The `value` field accepts either a URL or text content:
- If `value` is a valid URL, the block type is inferred (Image, Link, Embed, etc.)
- If `value` is plain text, a Text block is created

You can connect the block to multiple channels at once (up to 20).

Specify target channels using either `channels` (preferred) or `channel_ids` (legacy), but not both.
The `channels` form allows per-channel position and connection metadata.

**Authentication required.**

insert_at.description:
Position to insert the block in the channel (1-indexed).
Only valid when connecting to a single channel.

oneOf.description: Structured form: per-channel position and connection metadata

channels.description: Target channels with optional per-channel position and connection metadata.

value.value: This is a **text** block with markdown
value.title: My Text Block

403.description: Cannot add to one or more channels

post.description:
Queues multiple blocks for asynchronous creation and connects them to one
or more channels. Returns immediately with a `batch_id` that can be used
to poll for status via `GET /v3/blocks/batch/{batch_id}`.

Designed for import use cases such as migrating from other services
or re-importing Are.na exports.

Each block in the `blocks` array follows the same format as single block
creation. Blocks are processed sequentially in the background and partial
success is supported — if some blocks fail, the successful ones are still
created.

**Limits:**
- Maximum 50 blocks per request
- Maximum 20 channels per request
- All channels must be **private**

**Batch results are available for 24 hours after submission.**

**⚠️ Premium Only**: This endpoint requires a Premium subscription.

403.description: Forbidden — requires Premium subscription, or cannot add to one or more channels

get.description:
Returns the current status of a batch block creation job.
Poll this endpoint to track progress and retrieve results.

Batch results are available for 24 hours after submission.

**⚠️ Premium Only**: This endpoint requires a Premium subscription.

parameters.name: batch_id
parameters.description: The batch ID returned from the batch create endpoint

get.description: Returns detailed information about a specific block by its ID. Respects visibility rules and user permissions.

put.description:
Updates a block's metadata. Only the block owner can update it.

**Authentication required.**

content.description: Text content (for Text blocks only, supports markdown)

alt_text.description: Alt text for images (for Image blocks)
alt_text.example: A descriptive alt text for accessibility

metadata.description:
Custom key-value metadata. Uses merge semantics: new keys are added,
existing keys are updated, keys set to null are removed.

get.description:
Returns paginated list of channels where this block appears.
This shows all channels that contain this block, respecting visibility rules and user permissions.

200.description: List of channels where this block appears

get.description:
Returns paginated list of comments on this block.
Comments are ordered by creation date (ascending by default, oldest first).

200.description: List of comments on this block

post.description:
Creates a new comment on a block.

**Authentication required.**

You can mention users in the comment body using `@username` syntax.

delete.description:
Deletes a comment. Only the comment author can delete their comments.

**Authentication required.**

post.description:
Creates a new channel owned by the authenticated user or a group they belong to.

**Authentication required.**

group_id.description:
Group ID to create the channel under. The authenticated user must be a member of the group.
If not provided, the channel is owned by the authenticated user.

metadata.description: Custom key-value metadata to set on the new channel.

get.description: Returns detailed information about a specific channel by its ID or slug. Respects visibility rules and user permissions.

put.description:
Updates an existing channel. Only provided fields are updated.

**Authentication required.**

description.description: Channel description (supports markdown). Pass null to clear.

metadata.description:
Custom key-value metadata. Uses merge semantics: new keys are added,
existing keys are updated, keys set to null are removed.

delete.description:
Deletes a channel. This can not be undone.

**Authentication required.**

post.description:
Connects a block or channel to one or more channels.
Returns the created connection(s).

Specify target channels using either `channels` (preferred) or `channel_ids` (legacy), but not both.
The `channels` form allows per-channel position and connection metadata.

**Authentication required.**

connectable_id.description: ID of the block or channel to connect.

position.description:
Position to insert at within the channel (1-indexed).
Only valid when connecting to a single channel.

oneOf.description: Structured form: per-channel position and connection metadata

channels.description: Target channels with optional per-channel position and connection metadata.

get.description:
Returns detailed information about a connection,
including abilities (whether the current user can remove the connection).

The connection ID is included in the `connection` object when blocks/channels
are returned as part of channel contents.

put.description:
Updates a connection's metadata. Uses merge semantics: new keys are added,
existing keys are updated, keys set to null are removed.

**Authentication required.**

metadata.description:
Custom key-value metadata. Uses merge semantics: new keys are added,
existing keys are updated, keys set to null are removed.

delete.description:
Removes a block or channel from a channel by deleting the connection.
The block/channel itself is not deleted - it may still exist in other channels.

**Authentication required.**

post.description:
Moves a connection to a new position within its channel.
Requires sort permission on the channel.

**Authentication required.**

position.description: Target position, 1-indexed (required when movement is insert_at)

get.description:
Returns paginated contents (blocks and channels) from a channel.
Respects visibility rules and user permissions.

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: Filter by user who added the content

get.description:
Returns paginated list of channels where this channel appears.
This shows all channels that contain this channel, respecting visibility rules and user permissions.

200.description: List of channels where this channel appears

get.description:
Returns paginated list of users who follow this channel.
All followers are users.

200.description: List of users who follow this channel

get.description: Returns the currently authenticated user's profile
Returns detailed information about a specific user by their slug. Includes user profile, bio, and counts.

Returns paginated contents (blocks and channels) created by a user.
Uses the search API to find all content added by the specified user.
Respects visibility rules and user permissions.

Returns paginated list of users who follow this user.
All followers are users.

200.description: List of users who follow this user

get.description:
Returns paginated list of users, channels, and groups that this user follows.
Can be filtered by type to return only specific followable types.

200.description: List of users, channels, and groups that this user follows

get.description:
Returns paginated list of groups the user belongs to (as owner or member).
When authenticated as the target user, includes private groups.
Otherwise only public groups are returned.

200.description: List of groups the user belongs to

get.description: Returns detailed information about a specific group by its slug. Includes group profile, bio, owner, and counts.

Returns paginated contents (blocks and channels) created by a group.
Uses the search API to find all content added by the specified group.
Respects visibility rules and user permissions.

Returns paginated list of users who follow this group.
All followers are users.

200.description: List of users who follow this group

get.description:
Search across blocks, channels, users, and groups.

**⚠️ Premium Only**: This endpoint requires a Premium subscription.

**Examples:**
- Simple: `/v3/search?query=brutalism`
- Images only: `/v3/search?query=architecture&type=Image`
- My content: `/v3/search?query=*&scope=my`
- In a channel: `/v3/search?query=design&channel_id=12345`
- By a user: `/v3/search?query=*&user_id=456`
- PDFs sorted by date: `/v3/search?query=*&ext=pdf&sort=created_at_desc`

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
The search query string. Supports full-text search across titles,
descriptions, and content. Use `*` as a wildcard to match everything
(useful when filtering by type, scope, or extension).

parameters.name: type
parameters.description:
Filter results by content type. Accepts comma-separated values.
- Block subtypes: `Text`, `Image`, `Link`, `Attachment`, `Embed`
- Aggregate types: `Block` (all block types), `Channel`, `User`, `Group`
- `All` returns everything (default behavior)

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: Limit search to a specific context.

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: Limit search to a specific user's content.

parameters.name: group_id
parameters.description: Limit search to a specific group's content.

parameters.name: channel_id
parameters.description: Limit search to a specific channel's content.

parameters.name: ext
parameters.description:
Filter results by file extension. Accepts comma-separated values.
Only applies to Attachment and Image block types. Common extensions
include: pdf, jpg, png, gif, mp4, mp3, doc, xls, etc.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description:
Sort by relevance, date, name, or popularity. Defaults to `score_desc`.
Use `random` with `seed` for reproducible random ordering.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description:
Filter to only return results updated after this timestamp.
Useful for incremental syncing or finding recently modified content.
Format: ISO 8601 datetime string.

parameters.name: seed
parameters.description:
Random seed for reproducible random ordering. Only used when
`sort=random`. Providing the same seed will return results in
the same order, useful for pagination through random results.

details.message: This endpoint requires a Premium subscription

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description:
OAuth 2.0 authentication endpoints. Use these to obtain access tokens
for API requests. Register your application at [are.na/oauth/applications](https://www.are.na/oauth/applications).

**Endpoints:**
- Authorization: `https://www.are.na/oauth/authorize` (user-facing, on main site)
- Token: `https://api.are.na/v3/oauth/token` (API endpoint)

**Supported Flows:**
- Authorization Code: For web/mobile apps with a backend server
- Authorization Code + PKCE: For public clients (mobile apps, SPAs)
- Client Credentials: For server-to-server integrations

PKCE ([RFC 7636](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7636)) is recommended for
all authorization code flows and required for public clients.

tags.name: System
tags.description: Health checks and API metadata (OpenAPI spec, ping)

tags.name: Blocks
tags.description:
Blocks are the fundamental content units on Are.na. They can be text,
images, links, attachments, or embedded media. Blocks can be connected
to multiple channels and have comments.

tags.name: Comments
tags.description: Comments are attached to blocks. Users can add and delete their own comments.

tags.name: Connections
tags.description:
Connections link blocks or channels to channels. When you add a block to a
channel, you create a connection. Connections can be retrieved individually
or as part of channel contents.

tags.name: Channels
tags.description:
Channels are collections of blocks and other channels. They can be public,
private, or closed (viewable but not editable). Channels have an owner
(User or Group) and can have collaborators.

tags.name: Users
tags.description:
Users are individual accounts on Are.na. Each user has a profile with
channels, followers, and content they've created or saved.

tags.name: Groups
tags.description:
Groups are collaborative organizations that can own channels. Members
of a group can contribute to group-owned channels.

tags.name: Uploads
tags.description:
Direct file upload support via presigned S3 URLs. Use the presign endpoint
to get a temporary upload URL, PUT your file there, then create a block
with the resulting URL.

tags.name: Search
tags.description:
Full-text search across blocks, channels, users, and groups. Supports
filtering by type, user, channel, and various metadata fields.

**⚠️ Premium Only**: Search endpoints require a Premium subscription.
